search_parameters.q: how to treat a minor burn

search_information.query_displayed: how to treat a minor burn

text_blocks.answer: To treat a minor (first-degree) burn, immediately cool the area with cool—not cold—running water for 10 to 15 minutes. Gently remove jewelry or tight clothing, apply a thin layer of petroleum jelly, and cover it with a sterile, nonstick bandage. Take over-the-counter pain medication as needed.
For complete care instructions, follow these steps and precautions:
What to Do:
What to Avoid:

Consult a doctor or visit an urgent care facility if the burn is larger than the palm of your hand, is located on the face, hands, feet, or groin, or if you notice signs of infection (such as pus, spreading redness, or fever).
For detailed first-aid guidelines, refer to the Mayo Clinic Burn First Aid Guide or the American Burn Association.

organic_results.title: Burns: First aid
organic_results.snippet: You can usually treat minor burns at home: Cool the burn. Run cool — not cold — water over the area for about 10 minutes. If the burn is on the ...

organic_results.title: Minor burns - aftercare: MedlinePlus Medical Encyclopedia
organic_results.snippet: Clean the burn gently with soap and water. · Do not break blisters. · You may put a thin layer of ointment, such as petroleum jelly or aloe vera, ...
snippet_highlighted_words: Clean the burn gently with soap and water

organic_results.title: How to treat a first-degree, minor burn
organic_results.snippet: Cool the burn. Immediately immerse the burn in cool tap water or apply cold, wet compresses. Do this for about 10 minutes or until the pain subsides.
snippet_highlighted_words: Immediately immerse the burn in cool tap water or apply cold, wet compresses

organic_results.title: Burn First Aid
organic_results.snippet: First Aid for Minor Burns · Cool the burn with running water for at least 5 minutes (not cold or icy water). · Remove jewelry, rings, watches, and any clothing ...
snippet_highlighted_words: Cool the burn with running water for at least 5 minutes

organic_results.title: First aid tips for treating minor burns
organic_results.snippet: DO cool the burn immediately. Run cool (not cold) water over the area for at least five minutes. · DO remove any jewelry or clothing. · DO ...

organic_results.title: Ouch! I've burned my hand on a hot pan — now what?
organic_results.snippet: Place the burned area under running water slightly colder than room temperature for 10 to 15 minutes or until the pain eases. Or put a cool, ...

organic_results.title: First aid and treatment of minor burns - PMC - NIH
organic_results.snippet: The wound should be cleansed twice daily with mild diluted chlorohexidine solution. The burn should be covered with a bland ointment such as liquid paraffin.
snippet_highlighted_words: The wound should be cleansed twice daily with mild diluted chlorohexidine solution

organic_results.title: Burns and scalds
organic_results.snippet: You should treat all burns and scalds straight away. Hold the burn or scald under cool running water for 15 to 30 minutes, or until the pain feels better.
snippet_highlighted_words: Hold the burn or scald under cool running water for 15 to 30 minutes

related_questions.question: What ointment is best for a minor burn?
related_questions.answer: You may put a thin layer of ointment, such as petroleum jelly or aloe vera, on the burn. The ointment does not need to have antibiotics in it. Some antibiotic ointments can cause an allergic reaction. Do not use cream, lotion, oil, cortisone, butter, or egg white.Sep 4, 2024
